description: Fixed-size chunking cuts sentences or separates a claim from its context, so no single chunk contains a complete answer.
body: Failure mode: documents are split on a character count with no overlap, slicing a claim away from the qualifier or context that makes it interpretable, so retrieval returns fragments that cannot ground an answer. Detection signals: chunk boundaries fall mid-sentence; no overlap; answers that span a boundary retrieve incomplete passages; a boundary-spanning query test is missing. Do not split on raw length ignoring structure. Safer fallback: split on semantic/structural boundaries with overlap, size to the embedding range, and test that boundary-spanning answers still retrieve a self-contained chunk.
